middleware.description: Customer-facing scope guard. Applied to /api/v1/invoices/mine/* and /api/v1/credit-notes routes when the caller lacks invoice.invoices.viewAny. Auto-scopes queries: adds a WHERE clause `customer_id IN (auth.user.id, athlete_ids_where_user_is_guardian)` to the underlying repository. This makes customer routes safe by default — a customer cannot see another customer's invoices even by ID-guessing. Returns 404 (not 403) for out-of-scope IDs to avoid leaking existence.

consumed.note: Applied per-route with the specific entitlement key. All routes carry entitlement.enforce:invoice_capture (master); credit-note routes additionally carry entitlement.enforce:credit_note_issuance.
The permission-based authorize middleware wraps the ability checks defined in policies.json + permissions.json. Route-level `permission` metadata resolves through this middleware.

Not directly applied — invoicing is a contractual necessity (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)) that does not require consent. Consent module is invoked indirectly via notifications module for delivery-channel opt-out (but invoicing notifications carry consent_tier='transactional_required' and cannot be opted out).

Three throttle groups: invoice-customer (60/min per customer — reading own invoices), invoice-admin (600/min per admin — CRUD workflow), invoice-reports (30/min per admin — report queries are expensive), invoice-platform (100/min per platform_admin).
